description: Backfill the warranted-but-absent closure-table edges that early tasks' filed text entails but that were never written — the concrete instances of FGAP-091's under-filing. The relation-graph completeness audit (analysis/2026-06-14-substrate-relation-graph-completeness-audit.md) enumerated: TASK-010 task_governed_by_decision -> DEC-0008 and task_addresses_gap -> FGAP-012; TASK-012 task_governed_by_decision -> DEC-0009 and task_addresses_gap -> FGAP-015; TASK-014 task_addresses_gap -> FGAP-014. First re-confirm the complete warranted-but-absent set via find-references against the items' filed text (including any symmetric sibling such as TASK-011), then write each absent edge via append-relation — each kind-correct, accepted by the write-time validator. Pure data backfill, no code. Partial addressing of FGAP-091 (the concrete missing edges; the forcing-function invariant is FGAP-091's separate residual).
acceptance_criteria: The complete warranted-but-absent edge set is re-confirmed via find-references against the early tasks' filed text — the five audit-enumerated edges plus any symmetric sibling (e.g. TASK-011) — and every confirmed absent-but-entailed edge is included.

Each absent edge is written via append-relation and accepted as kind-correct: TASK-010->DEC-0008 (task_governed_by_decision), TASK-010->FGAP-012 (task_addresses_gap), TASK-012->DEC-0009 (task_governed_by_decision), TASK-012->FGAP-015 (task_addresses_gap), TASK-014->FGAP-014 (task_addresses_gap), plus any sibling confirmed in criterion 1.
find-references on each affected task shows the new edge(s); context-validate is warnings-only with no new issue beyond the pre-existing baseline.
